compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Why

The README is a thorough ~31KB reference (Why / Requirements / Install / CLI /
Python / MCP / Config / Tests) but has **no short tutorial** — Install jumps
straight into reference-style command docs. A newcomer has no golden path from
zero to a working first flow. Meanwhile `rebar --help` already emits a full,
authoritative command index backed by 40 per-command help files, so a separate
"cheat sheet" would only duplicate it and invite drift.

## What

Add a single, single-sourced `## Quickstart` section near the top of `README.md`:

- Install (one canonical line) -> `rebar init` -> create a first ticket ->
  `ready` / `claim` -> close, showing **expected output** at each step.
- A minimal "point an agent at it" example (the MCP one-liner) so the agent path
  is visible.
- Uses only the ~6 core commands inline and **defers the full command list to
  `rebar --help`** rather than re-listing commands.

## Scope

`README.md` only (one new section + a link to it from the top). Explicitly NOT a
separate cheat-sheet doc and NOT a duplicate command index — `rebar --help` +
the `_cli/help/*.txt` files remain the authoritative command reference. Keep it
short (a screenful).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `README.md` has a `## Quickstart` section near the top with an
  install -> first-ticket -> first-agent-flow walkthrough and expected output.
- [ ] It shows only the core commands inline and points to `rebar --help` for the
  full list (no re-listed command index, no separate cheat-sheet file).
- [ ] The commands shown are copy-pasteable and correct against the current CLI.
- [ ] A top-of-README link points to the Quickstart.
compiled_state.title: Add a single-sourced README Quickstart (golden path; defers to rebar --help)
